fields.body:
## Nested Workspaces

The memory-viewers target composes local README rules with child summaries from the nested memory-api and viewer-api workspaces. That makes this repo the concrete proof that nested workspace discovery and target composition work end to end.
fields.title: Nested Workspaces

fields.body:
## Repository Layout

- `ticket-viewer/`: browser UI for ticket workflows and graph review.
- `spec-viewer/`: browser UI for spec browsing and code-reference inspection.
- `memory-api/`: storage crates plus CLI, MCP, HTTP, and editor-facing tools.
- `viewer-api/`: viewer-facing shared APIs and control surfaces.
- `.rule/`: parent documentation workspace that can compose child workspace rules from nested repos.

The point of this repository is not just to store code. It is the top-level user map for the viewer stack: visual tools live here, deeper automation lives in `memory-api`, and nested rule workspaces let the READMEs stay generated from the repos that own the behavior.
fields.title: Nested Workspaces

fields.title: Nested Workspaces

fields.body:
## Tool Use Examples

```bash
viewer-ctl start ticket-viewer
viewer-ctl start spec-viewer
cargo run -p rule-cli -- sync-targets --config memory-viewers/rule-targets.yaml
```

- Start `ticket-viewer` to inspect work in progress and board relationships.
- Start `spec-viewer` to browse spec structure and linked code references.
- Regenerate the parent README when rule content changes.
